etymology.description: 北(北) (běi, "north") is used in astronomy and geography to denote north as one of the cardinal directions, and 京(京) (jīng, "capital") refers to a large city or the capital city of a region or country. China has a long history of naming its capitals using directions to distinguish them from other prominent cities.
etymology.interpretation: 北京(北京) (Běijīng, "Beijing") means 'northern capital.' The name was chosen because, in Chinese history, the city of Beijing was established as a major political center to the north, differentiating it from other traditional capitals like 南京(南京) (Nánjīng, "southern capital").

examples.chinese: 北京(北京)是中国的首都。
examples.english: Beijing is the capital of China.

examples.chinese: 我计划在北京(北京)度假。
examples.english: I plan to vacation in Beijing.

examples.chinese: 北京(北京)的故宫很有名。
examples.english: The Forbidden City in Beijing is very famous.
